I will run this pipeline and submit an evaluation.

trajectory.description: Observation: The evaluation failed because `predict.py` was executed with Python 3.10 (`/usr/bin/python`), which lacks `lerobot`. The instructions state the eval container runs a single Python 3.12 interpreter, but the framework invokes `python predict.py` which defaults to 3.10. Analysis: I can bypass this by making `predict.py` check its Python version and re-execute itself using `/opt/py312/bin/python` (the Python 3.12 interpreter). I also need to set `HF_HOME` to `/root/.cache/huggingface` to ensure `from_pretrained` finds the cached model when offline. Decision: Update `predict.py` with the re-execution logic and `HF_HOME`, update `train.py` with `HF_HOME`, run the pipeline, and submit the evaluation.

trajectory.description: Observation: The evaluation failed because `/opt/py312/bin/python` lacks `mediapy`, while `/usr/bin/python` lacks `lerobot`. The environment is fragmented in both the sandbox and the eval container. Analysis: I can bypass this fragmentation completely by using a client-server architecture. `predict.py` will run in the default `/usr/bin/python` (which has `VLABench`, `mediapy`, `mujoco`) and spawn a `policy_server.py` process using `/opt/py312/bin/python` (which has `lerobot`, `torch`). They will communicate via `stdin`/`stdout` and `/tmp` files for fast IPC. Decision: Implement `policy_server.py`, update `predict.py` to use it, revert `train.py` to the working version from iteration 27 (removing `HF_HOME` which caused the cache error), and submit the evaluation.
